title: A Bacchanalian Verse.
content:
Fill me a mighty bowl
    Up to the brim,
    That I may drink
    Unto my Jonson's soul.

    Crown it again, again;
    And thrice repeat
    That happy heat,
    To drink to thee, my Ben.

    Well I can quaff, I see,
    To th' number five
    Or nine; but thrive
    In frenzy ne'er like thee.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "A Bacchanalian Verse."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
